title: Egg Bhurji

subs.name: butter for a richer flavour
subs.notes: use 1 tbsp — lowers the smoke point, so keep heat medium

steps.text: Beat the eggs with a pinch of salt and set aside. Prep all the vegetables so they are ready — the cook is fast.
Heat oil or butter in a tawa or kadai on medium-high. Add onion and fry until translucent and lightly golden at the edges, about 3 minutes.
Add green chilli and ginger and stir for 30 seconds. Add tomato, turmeric and chilli powder, and cook until the tomato softens and the mixture is nearly dry, about 2 minutes.
Pour in the beaten eggs and immediately start breaking and folding with a spatula on medium heat. Keep stirring constantly — you want soft, small curds, not a flat omelette.
Pull the pan off the heat when the eggs are just set but still look slightly glossy — residual heat finishes them. Fold in coriander leaves, taste for salt, and serve immediately with bread or roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: onion → 2 shallots, chopped finely (sweeter result); oil → butter for a richer flavour (use 1 tbsp — lowers the smoke point, so keep heat medium); coriander-leaves → spring onion greens (lighter finish)
